name: Pipecat
pricing_notes: Open source; Daily.co (founders) offers hosted infrastructure; third-party transports supported

auth.notes: Framework-level auth-free; individual transports (Daily, Twilio, WebRTC) need their credentials

performance.notes: Real-time voice pipeline latency depends on STT/LLM/TTS components; framework adds ~5ms overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Real-time voice AI framework. Pipeline: audio input → VAD → STT → LLM → TTS → audio output. Supports Deepgram, OpenAI Whisper, ElevenLabs, Cartesia. Bot runner pattern. WebRTC/WebSocket transport. MCP server available.

gotchas: Real-time audio pipeline has many moving parts — debugging complex
Async pipeline with bidirectional audio requires careful state management
Requires assembling multiple third-party services (STT, TTS, transport)
best_when: Building voice AI bots (phone agents, real-time conversation systems)
Need end-to-end voice pipeline with swappable components
WebRTC-based voice applications needing LLM integration
Real-time AI voice agents for customer service automation
avoid_when: Simple chatbot without real-time audio requirements
summary: Pipecat is an open-source real-time voice AI framework from Daily.co. Builds end-to-end voice pipelines: VAD → STT → LLM → TTS with swappable components (Deepgram, Whisper, ElevenLabs, Cartesia, etc.). Best open-source option for building voice AI agents. More complex to deploy than managed alternatives (Vapi, Retell) but complete control over the stack.
